name: Linaria (Zero-Runtime CSS-in-JS)

performance.notes: Zero runtime — CSS extracted to static files at build

agent_readiness.notes: Zero-runtime CSS-in-JS. styled and css tagged template literals. Babel plugin extracts CSS at build time to .css files. css`color: red; font-size: ${fontSize}px`. styled.div`background: ${props => props.primary ? 'blue' : 'white'}`. CSS variables for dynamic values (not class injection). @linaria/react for React integration. Webpack/Vite/Babel plugin. Also: Pigment CSS (MUI's evolution of Linaria). Better performance than styled-components/emotion.

gotchas: Dynamic styles limited to CSS custom properties (variables)
Complex dynamic styling harder than runtime CSS-in-JS
best_when: Performance-critical React apps wanting styled-components syntax without runtime
SSR apps where CSS-in-JS runtime cost problematic
Teams migrating from styled-components wanting faster alternative
MUI v7+ apps (Pigment CSS is MUI's Linaria-based system)
avoid_when: Heavy dynamic styling (runtime emotion/styled-components more flexible)
Small apps where styled-components overhead acceptable
summary: Linaria is a zero-runtime CSS-in-JS library using tagged template literals. styled.div and css`` compile to static CSS files via Babel plugin. Dynamic values via CSS custom properties. No runtime JS for styling. Better SSR performance than emotion/styled-components. MUI's Pigment CSS is built on Linaria. Good choice when you want styled-components API with zero runtime cost.
